title: External Monitor Not Detected
cardText: Diagnose external monitors that are not detected or do not display an image.
Check monitor power, cables, input source, display settings, drivers, docking stations, and graphics hardware.
Separate monitor, cable, software, and graphics hardware issues.
text: This guide is for computers where an external monitor is not detected or does not display correctly.
Confirm the monitor and cable before investigating software settings.
Check display detection, graphics drivers, docking stations, and hardware connections.
Escalate hardware faults after basic troubleshooting is complete.

checkMonitorPower.title: Check monitor power
body: Confirm the monitor is turned on.
Check for a power indicator light or monitor menu.
A monitor with no power cannot be detected.

restoreMonitorPower.title: Restore monitor power
body: Reconnect the monitor power cable.
Try another power outlet if necessary.
Retest after the monitor powers on.

checkInputSource.title: Check monitor input source
body: Verify the monitor is set to the correct input.
Common inputs include HDMI, DisplayPort, USB-C, DVI, and VGA.
The monitor must match the connected cable.

selectCorrectInput.title: Select correct input
body: The monitor was listening on the wrong input source.
Select the correct input and test again.

checkCableConnection.title: Check cable connection
body: Ensure the display cable is firmly connected at both ends.
Check for loose, damaged, or bent connectors.
Reconnect the cable and test again.

replaceCable.title: Replace display cable
body: The display cable appears damaged or unreliable.
Replace it with a known-good cable and retest.

testAnotherCable.title: Test another cable
body: Display cables can fail even when no damage is visible.
Use another known-good cable if available.
Retest monitor detection.

cableFaultFound.title: Cable fault identified
body: The monitor works with another cable.
Replace the original cable.

testAnotherPort.title: Test another display port
body: Try another HDMI, DisplayPort, USB-C, or graphics output.
A failed port can prevent display detection.
Restart the computer after changing ports if needed.

displayPortFault.title: Display port fault identified
body: The monitor works on another output.
The original display port may be faulty or disabled.

detectDisplays.title: Force display detection
body: Open display settings.
Use Detect Displays if available.
Check whether the monitor appears in display settings.

checkDisplayMode.title: Check display mode
body: Verify the monitor is not disabled.
Choose Extend, Duplicate, or Second Screen Only as appropriate.
A detected monitor may still appear blank if disabled.

checkDockingStation.title: Check docking station or adapter
body: If using a dock, hub, or USB-C adapter, disconnect it temporarily.
Connect the monitor directly to the computer if possible.
Some docks require drivers, firmware updates, or power.

testWithoutDock.title: Test without dock
body: Connect the monitor directly to the computer.
This helps determine whether the dock is responsible.
Retest display detection.

dockFaultFound.title: Dock or adapter issue found
body: The monitor works without the dock.
Update dock firmware, drivers, or replace the dock if necessary.

checkGraphicsDriver.title: Check graphics drivers
body: Update or reinstall graphics drivers.
Display detection issues can occur after driver corruption or failed updates.
Restart the computer after driver changes.

testAnotherMonitor.title: Test another monitor
body: Connect a different monitor or TV.
This helps separate monitor faults from computer faults.
Use the same cable if possible.

monitorFault.title: Monitor fault likely
body: Another monitor works correctly.
The original monitor is likely faulty or misconfigured.

hardwareEscalation.title: Graphics hardware diagnostics required
body: The monitor, cable, ports, settings, dock, and drivers have been tested.
Possible causes include graphics card faults, motherboard faults, USB-C controller issues, or firmware problems.
Escalate for hardware diagnostics.
